judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with precise file/line citations; Report 1 is marginally more complete (explicit prod-build fix and OIDC id-token gap), but they are comparable.

report_1.evidence: `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"` (`infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:2`) ... Change: delete the line. ... `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"` ... Change: delete the line ... remove `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK` entirely from prod ... `LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN` ... Change: delete the line

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:2` `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"` ... Should be `"false"`. ... `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"` ... Should be removed entirely (empty) ... the sink must be emptied

report_1.evidence: the frontend bundle shipped to production would have `https://staging-api.example.com` baked into it as `VITE_API_BASE_URL`, because it was built once with `--mode staging` and never rebuilt. This is precisely the "rebuild per environment; don't promote a build artifact wholesale" trap

report_2.evidence: the production deployment ships a frontend bundle whose API base URL is hardcoded to `https://staging-api.example.com`. If applied as-is, the production frontend would call the staging API ... there's no `.env.production` and no `--mode production` build step, so a prod-specific bundle is never produced by this pipeline as written.

report_1.evidence: No migration step anywhere in `deploy.yml`. The runbook (`docs/prod-runbook.md:6`) states "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but nothing in `deploy.yml` runs `alembic upgrade` ... don't trust "the pipeline succeeded" as proof the prod schema is current; this needs to be confirmed/added explicitly

report_2.evidence: No database migration step. `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but no step in `deploy.yml` runs alembic or any migration tool ... so even if a runner existed, the prod schema would not actually be created by them.

report_1.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:3` still has `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"` — copied verbatim from staging ... a stricter environment must get its own scoped trust condition, never an inherited one. ... should read `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production"`

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:1-4` instantiates the same module but with `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"` — **unchanged from staging** ... it was copied verbatim and never edited to say `environment:production`
